title: Define AI Cockpit Trust Layer work-item loop
outOfScope: All production implementation files for the Trust Layer upgrade

sources.path: user-provided/AI Cockpit Trust Layer Upgrade specification
sources.reason: Defines the eight proposed implementation PRs, goals, principles, acceptance criteria, risks, and non-goals.
Defines the Work Item lifecycle, preflight pause behavior, archive, PR, and close requirements.
Defines repository governance concepts and evidence boundaries.
Aligns terminology for Contracts, Summaries, Evidence, Cockpit Status, and lifecycle closure.

problemStatement: The Trust Layer specification needs to become an ordered, independently reviewable Work Item backlog with a repeatable lifecycle loop and two final operational Work Items.

riskAssessment.reason: This Work Item authors a plan only. Future implementation, release, and destructive deletion remain separate Work Items with their own Contracts and gates.

executionDecision.reason: The scope is limited to authoring and verifying the ordered Work Item plan.

preReviewWarnings: Review that release and execution-plan deletion remain separate gated Work Items and are not silently executed by the plan-authoring task.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The specification is decomposed into eight implementation Work Items matching PR1 through PR8.
Two final Work Items are appended: publish a new version, then delete the execution plan.
Every Work Item defines scope, out-of-scope boundaries, dependencies, acceptance, verification, evidence, lifecycle closure, and the transition to the next Work Item.
The loop states that it may continue automatically only after the current Work Item is archived, its PR is merged, and ai-close-work-item reports ready for next Work Item.
Release and deletion operations are explicitly marked as separate gated operations; the user-provided authorization is recorded as context but cannot bypass repository/provider safeguards.
guidelines: Keep the plan language-neutral and repository-governance focused.
Do not claim that a Work Item is complete without its required evidence and checks.
Do not remove existing Work Item records while authoring the plan.

intent.problem: The Trust Layer specification contains multiple dependent proposals but does not yet provide an operational Work Item sequence with explicit lifecycle handoffs, release closure, and final plan cleanup.
constraints: Preserve AI Cockpit's evidence-first, fail-closed governance workflow.
Keep implementation, release, and destructive deletion as separate review units.
Record the user's requested authorization without treating it as a bypass for repository or provider safeguards.
Do not modify existing archived Work Item evidence while authoring the plan.
intent.rationale: A dependency-ordered loop makes the Trust Layer work reviewable and allows the next Work Item to start only after the current item's evidence, PR, merge, and lifecycle closure are complete.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
